common.emptyConnectDatabase: Connect to a database from the File menu to browse its schema.
common.languageDescription: Interface language. Date and number formatting still follow your operating system.
common.errorPanelCrashed: Something went wrong rendering this panel.

sandbox.ribbon: SANDBOX · {{product}} — pre-release build, isolated from your stable install
sandbox.tooltip: You are running the canary sandbox build. Its on-disk state is isolated in {{dir}}; it shares the OS keychain with the stable install.

view.label: View
view.help: Drag panel tabs to rearrange. Drop outside the dock area to float a panel. Counts and sizes are engine estimates.

connections.bulkDeleteConfirm: Delete {{count}} connections? This cannot be undone.

connectionDialog.description: Credentials are stored in the OS keychain, never written to disk in plaintext.

fields.name: Name
fields.connectionStringHint: Built automatically from the fields above. The password is stored securely and is not embedded.
fields.connectionStringWarning: Editing the connection string by hand can introduce errors — double-check it. Use this for Atlas (mongodb+srv://), replica sets, or extra URI options the form doesn't cover.

ssh.localPortHint: Leave at 0 to auto-assign a free port.
ssh.unavailableForSqlite: SQLite files don't need a tunnel.
ssh.hostKeyPolicyHint: How HuginnDB decides whether to trust the server's SSH public key.
ssh.noTrustedFingerprint: No key trusted yet for this host:port.
ssh.forgetConfirm: Forget the saved SSH host key for {{hostPort}}?
ssh.forgetDone: Host key forgotten. The next connection will trust whatever the server presents (when the policy is "Trust on first use").
ssh.forgetNone: No host key was stored for this host.
ssh.unavailableForSrv: SSH tunnelling is not available for mongodb+srv:// connections (SRV resolves to multiple hosts). Use a direct mongodb://host:port URI to tunnel.

connectionDialog.managerDescription: Pick a connection on the left to edit it, or create a new one.
connectionDialog.duplicatePasswordHint: The password isn't copied — re-enter it before connecting.

connectPassword.title: Password required
connectPassword.description: The stored password for "{{name}}" could not be found. Enter it to connect.

adhocDriver.title: Which database driver?
adhocDriver.description: "{{name}}" was launched without --driver and no default driver is set. Pick the backend to connect with.
adhocDriver.note: Tip: set a default driver in Settings → General to skip this next time.

cliConnect.title: Open incoming connection
cliConnect.description: A new launch wants to open "{{name}}". Open it in this window, or in a new one?

feedback.title: Report or suggest
feedback.description: File a bug or a feature request to the project's GitHub tracker.
feedback.descPlaceholder: What happened, what you expected, steps to reproduce…
feedback.hintApi: The issue will be created directly on GitHub.
feedback.hintBrowser: Without a token, a pre-filled GitHub page opens for you to submit.
feedback.openedBrowser: Opened a pre-filled issue in your browser
feedback.noGithub: I don't have a GitHub account
feedback.openedMailClient: Opened a pre-filled email in your mail app

drop.title: Drop table {{name}}?
drop.description: This permanently removes the table and all of its data. This cannot be undone.

dropView.title: Drop view {{name}}?
dropView.description: This permanently removes the view definition. The underlying tables and their data are unaffected. This cannot be undone.

empty.title: Empty table {{name}}?
empty.description: This removes every row but keeps the table and its structure. This cannot be undone.
empty.dontAskAgain: Don't ask again when emptying a table

createDatabase.title: New database
createDatabase.description: Create a new database on this server.
createDatabase.createdSingleDb: Database "{{name}}" created. This connection is scoped to a single database — open a new connection (or a multi-database one) to browse into it.

createCollection.title: New collection
createCollection.description: Create a new collection in this MongoDB database.

selectDatabases.title: Databases to show
selectDatabases.description: Pick which databases appear in the explorer for this connection. Hidden databases are also skipped when prefetching, which speeds up connecting to servers with many databases.

dropDatabase.confirm: Drop database "{{name}}"? This permanently deletes the database and all of its data on the server. This cannot be undone.

importSql.title: Import .sql… (Beta)
importSql.confirm: Run {{count}} statement(s) from this file against the active connection? This can modify or delete data and cannot be undone.

importCollection.title: Import JSON…
importCollection.confirm: Insert the documents from this file into "{{collection}}"? Existing documents with the same _id will cause the import to stop.

security.title: Security
security.empty: This driver has no server-side user/permission model.

cellEditor.title: Cell editor
cellEditor.sideEmpty: Right-click a cell and choose "Open in side editor" to edit it here.
cellEditor.discardChangesConfirm: You have unsaved changes in the side editor. Discard them and switch to the clicked cell?

filter.title: Advanced filter
filter.description: Filter rows by one or more columns. All conditions must match (AND).
filter.empty: No conditions yet — add one below.

structure.rebuildBody: SQLite can't alter this in place, so the table will be recreated: a new table is built, the data is copied over, the old table is dropped and the new one renamed. Make sure you have a backup if the data matters.

view.dropRecreateNote: drops & recreates (no data loss — views hold none)
view.previewEmptyQuery: Write a SELECT to see a live preview of the view's columns and rows.

tabs.emptyOpenSomething: Open a table from the schema explorer, or create a new query tab.
tabs.emptyConnectFirst: Select or create a connection to begin.
tabs.detachedUnavailable: This tab is no longer available.

settings.title: Preferences
settings.description: Changes apply live. The preferences file lives in your platform's config directory; the dialog is also available with

defaultDriver.label: Default database driver
defaultDriver.desc: Driver used when a connection is created without picking one — CLI launches without --driver, and the New connection form. "Ask each time" prompts on CLI launches instead of guessing.

confirmDestructive.label: Confirm destructive actions
confirmDestructive.desc: Ask for confirmation before deleting rows, dropping tables, or other irreversible operations.

restoreTabs.label: Restore tabs when opening a connection
restoreTabs.desc: Reopen the SQL and data tabs you had open the last time you used this connection.

reconnectOnLaunch.label: Reconnect on launch
reconnectOnLaunch.desc: When the app starts, automatically reconnect to the connections that were open when you last closed it. Uses the credentials saved in your OS keychain; connections whose password isn't stored are skipped.

queryHistoryLimit.label: Query history size
queryHistoryLimit.desc: Maximum number of recent queries kept in the history panel.

cellEditorMode.label: Cell editor
cellEditorMode.desc: Where a large cell value opens when you expand it: a centered dialog or a docked right-side panel.

connectionGroupExpandMode.label: Connection groups
connectionGroupExpandMode.desc: How folder groups appear in the File menu and the connections manager: expanded, collapsed, or remembering each group's last state.

wordWrap.label: Soft-wrap long lines
wordWrap.desc: Wrap lines that exceed the viewport instead of horizontal scrolling.

minimap.label: Show minimap
minimap.desc: Tiny overview on the right edge of the editor.

formatOnPaste.label: Format on paste
formatOnPaste.desc: Apply Monaco's basic SQL formatter to pasted snippets.

nullDisplay.label: NULL display
nullDisplay.desc: String used to render SQL NULL values in the grid.

truncateLongTextAt.label: Truncate text at (chars)
truncateLongTextAt.desc: Cap how many characters a cell renders in the grid; the full value stays available in the preview/editor. 0 disables.

zebraStripes.label: Zebra stripes
zebraStripes.desc: Alternate row background for easier scanning.

stickyHeader.label: Sticky header
stickyHeader.desc: Pin column headers while scrolling vertically.

cellPreview.label: Cell preview panel
cellPreview.desc: Show the floating value preview when a cell is selected. Off keeps single-click as pure navigation.

schemaMetric.label: Schema tree metric
schemaMetric.desc: Number rendered next to each table name in the schema explorer.

tabAccentStyle.label: Tab accent style
tabAccentStyle.desc: How the active tab and any tab colour you assign are drawn.

bitDisplay.label: BIT display
bitDisplay.desc: How MySQL BIT columns are rendered in the grid.

appearance.builtinInfo: Built-in theme — edits will fork into a new custom theme.

shortcuts.conflict: Already used by {{action}}. Choose a different key.

mcp.intro: Expose the connections you choose below to an AI coding tool (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Antigravity, Codex, …) over the Model Context Protocol — opt-in per connection, with a per-connection write policy (read-only by default).
mcp.unavailable: Not found — this build wasn't installed from a packaged release, so the connector isn't bundled.
mcp.noConnections: No saved connections yet — add one first.
mcp.writePolicyHint: Write access is per connection. “Read-only” allows only reads; “Data” allows row changes (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE); “Full” also allows schema changes (CREATE/DROP/ALTER). Changes take effect without restarting the AI client. The client still asks you to approve each action, and every write is recorded in mcp-audit.log next to your profiles.
mcp.selectHint: Select at least one connection above.
mcp.fullGuide: Full setup guide (Codex, Claude Desktop, flags, tools)…

about.tagline: Minimal desktop database manager for PostgreSQL, MySQL and SQLite.
about.tabStateHint: A sibling {{file}} stores the open tabs and schema-tree expansion for each connection.

export.title: Export profiles
export.securityWarning: Passwords are encrypted with AES-256-GCM. Keep your passphrase safe — it cannot be recovered.

import.title: Import profiles
import.pickDescription: Select a HuginnDB profile export file (.json) to import.
import.passphraseRequired: This file contains encrypted passwords. Enter the passphrase used when it was exported.
import.conflictsDescription: {{count}} profile(s) share an ID with an existing one. Choose how to handle each:
import.needsPassword: {{count}} profile(s) were imported without a password. Open each connection to set a password before connecting.

console.emptyNoActivity: No backend activity yet. Run a query or open a connection.
console.emptyNoMatch: No entries match the current filter.

query.runAllTitle: Run every statement in order on one connection
query.noResults: Run a query to see results.

saved.title: Saved Queries
saved.emptyNone: No saved queries yet. Use the bookmark icon in a query tab to save the current SQL.

saveQuery.description: Saved locally to your machine. Available across all connections.
saveQuery.name: Name

update.toastDescription: Download and install it from inside the app — it will relaunch automatically when done.
update.readyDescription: Downloaded in the background — restart to apply it.
update.mcpSidecarWarning:
An AI tool (Claude Code, Cursor, …) currently has the MCP connector running. Continuing will close it to install the update — the tool will reopen it automatically the next time it's needed.

Continue with the update?
update.errorPermission: Update failed: Windows didn't grant permission to install it. Try again and accept the admin prompt, or install the update manually from the GitHub releases page.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Guides for getting the most out of HuginnDB.

mcp.title: MCP connector
mcp.description: Connect AI tools to your databases

1_10_0.tagline: View editing lands in the schema explorer, alongside fully customizable keyboard shortcuts and quicker grid actions.

viewsEditor.title: Views can now be created, edited and dropped
viewsEditor.body: The schema explorer's new "Edit view…" tab pairs a full Monaco SQL editor with a live preview grid and a read-only DDL pane — no more hand-writing CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW.

betweenFilter.title: A `between` operator in the Advanced Filter
betweenFilter.body: Filter an inclusive range in a single condition — on PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite and MongoDB — instead of stacking a >= row and a <= row.

shortcuts.title: Customizable keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.body: Settings → Shortcuts lets you rebind six actions — open settings, command palette, tab switcher, refresh, run query and the new expand-cell shortcut — with collision checks and a reset-all button.

gridQuickActions.title: Faster data-grid actions
gridQuickActions.body: Click a selected cell to reveal an expand icon for a quick full-value view, and use Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V to copy and paste cell values directly — no more double-clicking just to read.

1_9_0.tagline: Your AI tools can now write to your databases — safely, on your terms, one connection at a time.

mcpWrite.title: MCP connector write-mode
mcpWrite.body: The huginndb-mcp connector, read-only until now, can insert, update and delete rows — and change schema — through your AI tool. New insert_row, update_cell and delete_rows tools, plus writes via run_query.

mcpPolicy.title: Per-connection write policy, with an audit log
mcpPolicy.body: Each connection is read-only by default; raise it to 'data' or 'full' in Settings → MCP, per connection, with changes taking effect immediately. Whole-table UPDATE/DELETE without WHERE are refused, and every write is recorded in mcp-audit.log.

advancedFilter.title: Advanced per-column filter
advancedFilter.body: A new filter button in the table toolbar opens a builder: add conditions per column (contains, starts/ends with, >, <, equals, is null…), all combined with AND and applied on the server. Type-aware, and works across PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite and MongoDB.

1_8_0.tagline: MongoDB catches up: multi-database connections and the MCP connector both get real driver parity.

security.title: Security panel works on multi-database connections
security.body: Opening the Security tab on a MongoDB connection with no preselected database no longer errors — it now lists users and privileges across the whole cluster.

mcpMongo.title: MCP no longer blocks MongoDB reads
mcpMongo.body: `run_query` used a SQL-only read-only check that rejected every mongosh statement by default. It now recognises MongoDB reads correctly, the same way the desktop query editor already did.

mcpMongoDatabase.title: MCP tools can target a MongoDB database
mcpMongoDatabase.body: list_tables, describe_table, list_indexes, browse_table (schema) and run_query (database) can now target a specific database on a multi-database MongoDB connection — the same thing expanding a database in the explorer does, just from your AI tool.

columnTypes.title: Real column types instead of generic "bson"
columnTypes.body: Query and browse results now show each column's actual BSON type (int, string, date, objectId, …) instead of one generic label — useful for you and for AI tools using the MCP connector.

collectionSize.title: Collection size in the explorer
collectionSize.body: MongoDB collections now show an on-disk size, fetched in a single batched call instead of skipping it.

1_7_0.tagline: Bring your databases to your AI tools — HuginnDB now speaks the Model Context Protocol.

connector.title: MCP connector for AI tools
connector.body: A new headless `huginndb-mcp` server lets Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor and other MCP clients inspect your databases — schema, tables, indexes, sample rows and server info — using the connections you already saved.

safety.title: Read-only and opt-in by design
safety.body: Nothing is exposed until you name a connection, queries are read-only, passwords stay in your OS keychain, and a row cap keeps results in check. See docs/MCP.md to wire up your client.

1_6_0.tagline: A top-to-bottom interface overhaul — the same keyboard-first HuginnDB, sharper in every corner.

design.title: Refined design system
design.body: A consistent colour, type and elevation scale, clearer focus rings, and semantic success / warning tokens that follow your theme.

grid.title: Keyboard-navigable data grid
grid.body: Move the active cell with the arrow keys, select rows with visible checkboxes, and read numbers in aligned tabular figures.

schema.title: Redesigned schema tree
schema.body: The open table is marked so you always know where you are, column types are colour-coded, and rows load behind a subtle skeleton.

editor.title: Sharper query editor & command palette
editor.body: A visible Run button with its shortcut, a flagship cell editor with content-type badges, and a polished command palette with hints.

chrome.title: Clearer status & themed tooltips
chrome.body: Lost connections are unmissable with one-click reconnect, and tooltips across the chrome now match your theme instead of the OS.
